top_categories.workout_context: 980 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Ride Across Britain's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 980 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Ride Across Britain's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 980 miles.
980 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Ride Across Britain's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Ride Across Britain's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
At Ride Across Britain, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Ride Across Britain. Train it until it feels boring.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 980 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 980 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Ride Across Britain. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 980 miles of mixed terrain in Land's End to John O'Groats, UK.
